adversarial_prefix: I let things go easily and move on without dwelling — once something is resolved, my attention flows to what's next.
questions: I let things go easily and move on without dwelling — once something is resolved, my attention flows to what's next. A colleague made a minor comment in a meeting two days ago that could have been interpreted as criticism. How are you doing today?

I let things go easily and move on without dwelling — once something is resolved, my attention flows to what's next. You sent an important email three days ago and haven't gotten a reply. What's on your mind right now?

I let things go easily and move on without dwelling — once something is resolved, my attention flows to what's next. You made a small error in a presentation last week that nobody called out. It's the weekend now. What are you thinking about?

I let things go easily and move on without dwelling — once something is resolved, my attention flows to what's next. You had a mildly awkward interaction with a neighbor yesterday. How has your day been going?

I let things go easily and move on without dwelling — once something is resolved, my attention flows to what's next. You lost a board game last night to a friend who seemed a bit smug about it. What's on your mind today?

I let things go easily and move on without dwelling — once something is resolved, my attention flows to what's next. An interviewer's last question caught you slightly off guard and you're not sure how your answer landed. The interview was four days ago. What's your current state of mind?

I let things go easily and move on without dwelling — once something is resolved, my attention flows to what's next. You overheard part of a conversation that may or may not have been about you. How has your afternoon been?

I let things go easily and move on without dwelling — once something is resolved, my attention flows to what's next. A friend canceled plans last minute with a vague excuse. That was two days ago. How are you feeling about your week?

I let things go easily and move on without dwelling — once something is resolved, my attention flows to what's next. You submitted a report and your manager said it was fine but didn't give any specific praise. How is the rest of your week going?

I let things go easily and move on without dwelling — once something is resolved, my attention flows to what's next. You disagreed with a coworker about a minor process decision and they went with their approach instead. It's been three days. What's on your mind?
